paper_title: Molecular Dynamics Simulation of Dimer on Substrate

methods.description: Simulating the deterministic and thermal dynamics of a dimer moving on a periodic one-dimensional substrate using Langevin dynamics. The particle-substrate interaction is a sinusoidal function, and the interparticle interaction is a harmonic potential. The equations of motion are integrated using a velocity-Verlet algorithm, and trajectories are averaged over multiple realizations for thermal diffusion cases.

steps.description: Define the physical model including particle types (dimer atoms), substrate potential (sinusoidal function of amplitude 2U 0 and period a), and interparticle potential (harmonic potential with spring constant K and equilibrium length l).
steps.searchable_summary: Define physical model for dimer on 1D periodic substrate with specified potentials

steps.description: Incorporate Langevin dynamics into the equations of motion to account for finite temperature T, including a damping term mηẋ i and stochastically fluctuating forces f i satisfying < f i (t) >= 0 and < f i (t)f j (0) >= 2mηk B T δ ij δ(t).
steps.searchable_summary: Implement Langevin dynamics for finite temperature simulation

steps.description: Set specific representative parameter values for the simulation: a = 0.25 nm, U 0 = 0.2 eV, m = 5×10 −26 kg, η = 0.7 ps −1 . The values of K and l will be given in the caption of each figure.
steps.searchable_summary: Set simulation parameters for the defined model
inputs: Specific parameter values (a, U0, m, η, K, l)

steps.description: Choose and configure the integration algorithm, using a velocity-Verlet algorithm, with a time step ∆ = 10 −16 s.
steps.searchable_summary: Configure velocity-Verlet integration algorithm with specified time step

steps.description: Perform molecular dynamics (MD) simulations, integrating the equations of motion using the configured algorithm and time step.
steps.searchable_summary: Run molecular dynamics simulation to generate trajectories
inputs: Initial conditions (positions x1, x2 and velocities ẋ1, ẋ2)
outputs: Simulation trajectories (x1(t), x2(t), ẋ1(t), ẋ2(t) over time)

steps.description: Averaging the trajectories over several thousands of realizations in the case of thermal diffusion, in order to reduce the statistical noise.
steps.searchable_summary: Average simulation trajectories over multiple realizations
outputs: Averaged simulation results (e.g., mean position, velocity, diffusion coefficient)
